_description: Scene 3b: Ball delivery to human — NO obstacle avoidance (raw spring-damper)
_notes: Same geometry as Scene 3 (start/goal/obstacle positions identical).
Key difference: obstacle avoidance is NONE — no DMP repulsion and no
hard radial projector. The trajectory follows the raw spring-damper
attractor and may freely penetrate the obstacle zone.
HARD  → DMP repulsion + hard radial projector  (guaranteed, Scene 3)
SOFT  → DMP repulsion only                     (preferred, no guarantee)
NONE  → no repulsion, no projector             (raw behavior, Scene 3b)
The ObstacleAvoidance clause below has modality=PREFER (soft optimizer cost,
weight=6.0). This nudges the optimizer via PIBB but provides no geometric
enforcement — only the avoidance tier (NONE here) controls geometry.
2-phase task: carry ball to human (0-7s), hold at human position (7-10s).
No pour: ball stays upright, no orientation change needed.
Stiffness reduction near human is still active (same CGMS compiler cost).

clauses._comment: Reach goal position within carry phase
Hold position at human during hold phase
SOFT: nudge path away from obstacle but penetration is acceptable (harmless ball)
Prefer ball stays upright during carry but not hard-required
